list.item: comments on changes in the ministerial composition in Wallachia and notes the entry as minister of "Mr. Tudoraki's son"
for the first time a merchant became a minister in Wallachia
delivery in spring at the latest
"There is no revolution in Bulgaria. You saw what happened in the newspapers."
350 stuffing bags with a request to store in Almeny and send a sample
14 packages - first order and 28 packages - second
missing from the market in Romania
"Those from Greece must be careful about weight"
but they don't match the samples sent earlier "There must have been some kind of mess along the way. So come here to see them"
"Buffalo hides and silks have become so expensive
let's let others profit from them"
with a share of 3791 5/4 gr. for the addressee (D. H. Nitsu)
Arrived in Marseille "to take the job"
30 sacks of sugar were obtained. Request to send spawn
The iron and rum haven't arrived yet
Purchase of 1000 kg. from the port in Mamai
Transport prices to Zimnich and Gyurgevo
Interest of 60 money per % (not clear per month or per year)
He criticized them for buying at a higher price of the goods. The skins come from Uzundzhovo
The skins from Uzundzhovo are arriving
He is looking for his money on a policy worth 5,000 lei
To transfer the money from purchases and debtors
Insists on money transfers on expiring policies
that they accepted the skins from 08.12.

Distribution of Bulgarian calendars "Tomorrow with Mr. Marincha Benli, I will send you one knot of 200 # for yours in Galats and two packages with 100 calendars, Bulgarian, of which you should send to ours in Galats, and you should distribute you over there on our loved ones"
It is unclear where he sent the calendars (Braila
Bucharest). "I wish the case good success
in other words, with pure and Bulgarian thoughts towards our dear fatherland"

desc.persName: the boy of Iv. H. Dimitrov

list.item: reports how much he enjoys bread spirit
node EG №70 next to brothers x. Theologian in Galatians
policy withdrawals in pounds and charclies
Georgievi are interested in the following goods - black fine threads
one policy of 100 Turkish lira for the account of Karaminkov's money
G. Iokonomov and G. Tedelcho Petrovich
He sent his brother a node with 495 polyimperials
880 20/40 Wallachian groschi. The following goods are mentioned: sugar
tin. The prices of some commodities such as rice are indicated
Hristo bought Hristo Nedyalkovich's spirits yesterday
Yesterday, Hristo Georgiev received a letter from Stamati's brother in Vienna. It is about 2 policies of the brother in Vienna
It's about policies - two policies worth 10
800 groshis: "two of your policies meet G.H. Thedorais and M.L" - G.H. Todorais? и М. Леон"
Lazar bought 60 poods of pure wax for 15 rubles. He would have bought another 90 poods. Lazar wrote to Evlogi Georgiev to send him money for the payment of the wax
which was sent by Geshov. Another policy has been sent
which was supposed to be honored by Gos. Ilia "and at maturity I take care of the redemption and in time you will show me your will for them."
Karaminkovi decided to Hristo Georgiev to send Stefan Toshkovic to node 150 polyimperial

desc.persName: for Hristo Georgiev Nik. Hr. Puliev

list.item: The forwarding of letters for Karlovo to Pulievi
Iliya Stoyanov's policy was accepted and cashed at maturity. Another policy was accepted against Simeon Ivanov
who came to Bucharest two days ago from Galati. Hristo Georgiev takes care of redeeming it at maturity and. He forwards letters to them
because sales are weak. To send the goods either through Constantinople
or via Serbia via Raduevic. Don't send her through Belgrade and Zemon
The policy of 100 Russian poly-imperials against Kon is mentioned. D. Rusovich

Today, Hristo Georgiev will take care of the cashing of the policy of 100 Russian polyimperials, "that you gave me a meeting Cos. D. Rusovich. I am sending them a letter about Seres to Brothers M. Botti, asking you to direct it by post without delay. Those friends who are here have shown you some policy up to 2 thousand fiorins, accept them and withdraw the prices here for me to pay."
The bills have been received in connection with the sale of barley. once you check it out
will write. There is probably an error
The weather is bad and no hirers can be found to transport the wine
to collect and use for his work. To write to him how many liters of brandy were the 4 barrels
which he sent to Giurgevo. To come to Bucharest and bring all the bills

12 thousand gr were collected from his policy and the rest will be paid in 6 days. There is a policy from G. Tapchilestov in Tsarigrad for 16,000 gr. 7,000 gr. has been given, and the rest will be given after 6 days. From the accounts to the end of m. December the debt of P. Nedelkovich is 1248. 1 gr
To write in detail what they know about yesterday's battle. He will send them 10 barrels of the fine sugar. To write to him if there are customers with them for red and black caviar
to send them. Reports spawn prices
He sends him 11 barrels of powdered sugar and 4 crates of French olive oil
A letter has been delivered. He will write to Hristo Georgiev in Braille
that his goods were not sold. He advises him to speed up the sale of the goods
Sending them 482 half-imperials in a group of "your pounds sterling sold" today with the local mail via braille.
Sends him 3 glasses of soft drinks. He sends him 12 thousand groshis as soon as he finds a reliable person
He received the money sent - 18,806 groshis
with which he credited his account. Will convey to Vasilaki for the wine only to have tenants. Captain Chernov volunteered "and God willing
Two hirelings were sent from Gyurghevo with two empty barrels. To be filled with rakias to be sent to N. Andr. and G. Economu
No letter from him. Ask him to sell the goods. He must have collected money from the earlier sales of his goods and from the barley. For whatever money he has
He has received the money sent
of which he gave 1498 24/40 groshis to Smarantaki
and 458 1/2 for small purchases. The remaining 299 was deposited "into your account with me"
which he did not receive due to bad weather. Sends him a bunch of money "H.G. #74"
to forward to Evlogi Georgiev in Galats
He has received the 25 half-imperials he sent
of which he bought from Mr. Smaraitiki 64 litpi and 50 oki sakizi and grocers
If he finds customers with 24 grams per liter of brandy, he can sell up to 1000 liters. The rumor is spreading
that more Russian troops are arriving
to 30 thousand only cavalry. If that happens
they will sell the remaining amount of hay at a profit
because there is no account. There is no iron. There is only one
cut into larger pieces of 160 grossh each
He sent 200 Austrian zlotys to Braila. He sold 5 barrels of rum
but not to send of this commodity
because it is not wanted here
one node of 88 20/fanga gold was sent
to also send the bill to the chaplams
1000 tori threads black good from Trapeze

desc.persName: G. kha Nik. kha Kostov and br.

list.item: Nikilaki Kantardzhiyat and Atanas Minkovich negotiated in Svishtov as much as they could find
"Our Bulgarians don't like to buy books
and this, I dare to say, is the main reason why we are not progressing in enlightenment..."
"Mr. Geshov's work in Vienna should be taken over by his brother Ivancho"
Comments for buyers from London and Marseille, but with unfavorable offers
300 French francs sent to Rakovski by Hristo Georgiev
300 French francs sent to Rakovski by Hristo Georgiev
Condolences on the death of Hristo Geshov
Two pishtovs "with 6 ognyuvi ... I will prepare them for you today and tomorrow and I will send them to you with the fainting mail"
details of completed and future transactions
Looking for sellers of the scaffolding in Oltenica
a case between Gitza and Pashaliou
100 barrels of sugar of 330 kg each for Galats and Braila
a dispute over the price of rice
looking for a machinist from Vienna
where I have been able to serve you"
"I come for the last time to invite you"
that if I also save myself in my case
"make it easier for him from there with something for his expenses
"and I am forced to take unpleasant measures"
"Your Alexander is healthy (...) he comes to me every Sunday and that
I advise him too. He is a good boy and shy
and shy people always give hope."
price information in London and Marseille
that they continue to withdraw money from Tapchilestov
that he no longer keeps money with him
"all his work is criminal and I hope to rob him"
"...let's get rid of this black stone Greek
He wants a document from Načović regarding the case with Sishmanović
"of all their enterprises there shall be loss
that's why he doesn't mind his work
I think it comes from inattention"

desc.persName: D. H. Nichov i Iv. Todorov
D. H Nichov i Iv. Teodor.
D H Nichov i Ivam Teol
D. H. Nichov i Iv. Teodoru

list.item: Mr. Cv[yatko] Raevich is ill "on the brink"
advice on how to remove the goods before they are handed over to the tenants

desc.persName: for Hristo Georgiev - Nikola(y) Hr. Pulevich

list.item: There is no money from Constantinople
"bring them to me without the slightest delay
for otherwise I shall draw them back also, and which will be quite unprofitable for you
but there's nothing I can do to you"
"to be clean as a mirror and work like a clock"
to tell the editor Mihailovski "to run away from foreign languages ​​and use purely Bulgarian"..."Galatz
they could have waited for a better price
as well as the wheat of Eulogius of Galatians
are looking for Bessarabian buyers for the Sucieshti sheep
correct an error in previous general accounts
Georgiev failed to harvest corn, to send him
versions connected with trade with London
the sample was sent by courier to Cullerash
waiting for a barge from the steamship agency
mentions prices in London and Marseilles
the sheep and cows will pass under the management of P. Dimitrescu
directions for the mission from Tatina to be sought from Mr. Kostaki
except a bit old and holey"
mention prices in London and Marseilles
criticizes Pulievi for not informing him about the sale of tobacco to Constantinople through Tapchilestov
trade in "9 bales of plain yarn"
the yarn was sold in association with traders in Galats
the total value of the transaction is 15317.33 gr.
the deal is again cooperative with entrepreneurs in Galats
trade in "6 bales of plain yarn"
the yarn was again sold in partnership with merchants from Galats
the yarn was again sold in partnership with merchants from Galats
the yarn is placed in partnership with traders from Galats
they were sent through Varna and Ruse from Selveliat
it is about 198 sacks of rice

The sacks of rice were delivered to Bucharest through the mediation of Mr. Hr. Sahatchiev in Zimnich. They were sold to various persons at the expense of Atanas Karakashev et al. the names of 16 buyers of the rice are indicated (eg Samuil Majara?
The cotton was delivered through the mediation of merchants in Giurgevo
The goods were delivered with the help of intermediaries in Gyurghevo
The manufacture was adopted by Gyurgevo
partnership deal with 90 edecs of chervish
The letter was forwarded through Mr. Nikolay Kantarjidi in Ruse
were sold in partnership with the brothers H. Theologou
the item was sent from manchester
сделка с 17 варела захар и 50 чувала кафе
partnership deal with brothers x. Theologian
they were delivered via Sahatchiev to Zimnich
partnership deal with brothers x. Theologian
they were delivered through Sahatchiev in Zimnich
a deal of 44 1/2 sacks of rice
on which the rice is placed
the rice was sent through Zimnich through the mediation of Hr. Sahatchiev and others.
the hoods are placed on three faces
the goods were delivered via Giurgevo
the goods were sent via Varna
a deal of 42 sacks of rice
the rice was sent through Hristo Sahatchiev and others. from "there" ie from Svishtov
the rice was sold to 9 persons
the prices of various goods are indicated - e.g. misiri
information on how food sales are going
deal with 49 edeks of chervish cooperative
purchased honey from Craiova via Petra Dyankova and sent via Zimnić via Petraki Teodoru for joint account
bought beans in three parts for a partnership account
which were sent through Mikhail Popovich to Braila
he sent 33 sacks of rice to Bucharest through the people of Svishtov, Hristo Popov and his team
cooperative trade in cow and beef vermicelli
deal with 66 edecs of cow and ox wormwood
sent by Mich. Avramov in Gyurghevo
sending a node with money to "Ours in Karlovo"
they were asked to send up to 100-150 sacks of rice to Galatz
Tapchilestov ordered the goods to be sent through G. Mavridi and brothers H. Petkovi from Ruse
while Hristo Georgiev insists that this be done through G. Nikola Kantardjidi
sends them a bundle of money
in a note after the letter is added
that the residents of Svishtov can take out a policy "for Tsarigrad to Hristo P. Tapchilestova up to 19 thousand groschi kaime"

desc.persName: Gospoda H. Petar i bratya Nedelovich

list.item: Mr. Kantarjidi will send them 20 edecs of wormwood. To send it by steamer to the Tapchilestov brothers in Constantinople. Hristo Tapchilestov wrote to Hristo Georgiev that he sent 20 boxes of mackerel, 26 sacks of soap and 51 tolums of oil in Varna to Georgievich, who were advised to forward the goods to Mavridi and brothers H. Petkovi in ​​Ruse. Hristo Georgiev advises Georgievich to send the goods through another friend of his in Ruse - Nikolay Kantardjidi. Georgievich to "notice" (i.e. report) the rents (i.e. the transport fee) of Hristo Georgiev's goods. If the rent from Varna to Tutrakan is lower, should the goods be forwarded there to Mr. Hristo Aleksandriev, who should be ordered to forward them to Oltenitsa, to Mr. Vasilaki Gitsa? under the order of Hristo Georgiev.

desc.persName: Gospodin Mavridi i Bratya H. Petkovi

list.item: If there is a white and clean sake in Ruse
to take at the current price in the market and send Hristo Georgiev 400/500 okas and send them without delay.
Hristo Georgiev sends him rice and cham sakaze from a settlement in Transylvania
Sending rice to Galatsi and Zimnich. Yamurluci are also mentioned

desc.persName: Gospodin At. N. Karakashtov i dr.

list.item: Ikonomov's skins have arrived safely in Vlasko and will be sold after the holidays

desc.persName: Gospodin Hristo Sahatchiev? /familnoto ime ne se chete yasno/

list.item: Sacks of rice are mentioned. The forwarding of letters in Svishtov
rice and cham sakaze are mentioned
rice and cham sakaze are mentioned
communicates detailed information about completed transactions
for the prices of goods - rice

desc.persName: For Hristo Georgiev signed Nikolay Hr. Puliev

list.item: 20 sacks of rice were sent from Bucharest - 2163 1/2 okas
to pay their rent to the tenant Dimitru Nesru Bucharestyan

desc.persName: Bratya Hristo i Nikola T. Pulievi

list.item: Rice purchased from Plovdiv is mentioned
Hristo Georgiev gives an order to buy more rice - 500/600 kilos
the renters arrived with 36 sacks of rice
a node with 340 polyimperials was sent
Yamurluci. To send a letter to Karlovo
Rice is mentioned. Hristo Georgiev will send them money - 200 polyimperials
Hristo Georgiev sends them 200 polyimperials. Commercial transactions in rice
"Mr. Mosha" - probably there is a predivd Mosho Ioanu
From Ruse, they provide information about Cham Sakaz. Information about the price of white and red wine
the sending of chervish to Varna
They were sent 322 polyimperials in a node. to forward it to At. N. Karakashev in Svishtov

Hristo Georgiev wrote to Ruse to buy 200 okis of cham sakaz, which Hristo Nedyalkovic should redirect to Bucharest. The letter and the telegram stated that Hristo Georgiev had telegraphed G. P. Teologu in Manchester to send 25 barrels of Amver sugar via Vienna
It has accepted a node with 35 polyimperials
information on the prices of goods - rice

desc.persName: Mr. Mavr. and Br. H. Petkovi

list.item: 28 barrels of Ortash wax are mentioned
the valuation of 50 barrels of Amver sugar is indicated
Evlogi intended to buy 800 ox hides at 110 groshis a pair
sale of mesiris. Mr. Mosho bought 360 kilos of mesiri at Georgievi's expense, i.e. corn
Hr. Georgiev indicates the prices of various goods - rice
Tapchilestov bought oil on Ortash principles with Georgievi. Hristo Georgiev points out
that butter is used by grocers in Bucharest
white drops. To G. Nikola Hr. Popov counted 7040 groschi. Mr. Naiden will conduct up to 1000 polyimperials
for an item to be purchased. Evlogi Georgiev sent a bundle of money to the L. Hamamdzhiev brothers
Accepted node with money according to brothers L. Hamamdzhiev. The prices of the furniture in Karlovo. Prices of goods in Bucharest. The bill of exchange for London is indicated
i.e. London. Mr. Theologou has sent samples of 4 types of sails. Ours in Kralovo / i.e. Nikola T. Puliev and Stefan Vassilev/

desc.persName: Bratya Hristo i Nikola T. Pulievi

list.item: Hristo Georgiev has sent to Karlovo 2 bags with 4000 5/taci Turkish according to Hr. Popov and others. for the purchase of mesh and rice. Evlogi Georgiev from Galats wants to buy him black and white sakhtians
Plovdiv. Hristo Georgiev reports the prices of goods in Bucharest

desc.persName: G. Mavridi and Br. H. Mavridi

list.item: Our Ivanchu has bought 80 tons of Sahtiyani

desc.persName: Bratya Hristo and Nikola T. Pulievi

list.item: two entries worth 5885 1/2 grosz are indicated. and 2942 1/2 grosz. Ours
3 sacks of wax and 1 deng tokat bars were sent
Hristo Georgiev received a bundle of money from his brother - 20,882 10/40 gr.
Hristo Georgiev indicated to his brother the prices of a number of goods
also indicates the bill of exchange for London
At the end of the letter, the price of the rice is stated

desc.persName: G. At. N. Karakashov and others

list.item: Did Hristo Georgiev send them 3,000 ikosar? through Geshovi. Will send them more
Hristo Georgiev sent them 3000 ikosar? Gešovi to hand them over to Ivana Teodoru of Tarnovec. He'll send them more money. Matea? Georgiou from here
Hristo Popov and others. they have rice and yamurluci in their farm - goods
property of Hristo Georgiev. Svishtovci to forward a letter in Karlovo to Pulievi
Submit a node for Dimitar H. Nichov et al. in Tarnovo

desc.persName: Mister Sotir Ikonomov and G. Tsenov
G. Mavr. and Brothers H. Petkovi
Gos. H. Nik. P. i sinove

list.item: At the end of the letter, the prices of some goods are announced.
The prices of the goods are reported.
At the end of the letter, the price of the silk is stated

desc.persName: Dimitur H. Nichov and Iv. Teodorovich

list.item: Hristo Georgiev asks the people of Svishtov to recommend good and honest friends from Nikopol
to buy from them 100/200 thousand oki of sumac
Hristo Georgiev asks him for information about him
buffalo and ox hides in these lands

desc.persName: G. Ned. Andr. and G. Ikonomu

list.item: Forwarding nodes with money to ours in Karlovo. Ours /meaning Pulievi in ​​Karlovo/
